rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the target component or implementation scope, following AADL structural rules
Preserve existing AADL architecture by inserting annex blocks at valid locations without modifying other elements
Use visible identifiers from the current context when available, but do not assume their existence
Avoid using subcomponent paths in component type annexes to maintain deterministic contracts
generic_rules: Insert annex blocks at the most specific valid scope that contains all referenced identifiers
Preserve existing component types and implementations when inserting annex blocks
Use visible identifiers from the current context when available, but do not assume their existence
Avoid using subcomponent paths in component type annexes to maintain deterministic contracts
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are the normal location for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Component implementation annexes are used for implementation-level bindings such as assignments when they are valid in that scope
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not encode AADL property associations as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
Do not assume the existence of identifiers not explicitly visible in the current context
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
